messages:
{"parts":[{"content":"These are candidate learnings that may have duplicates (same insight, different wording).

CANDIDATES:
[0] (personal) Task initiation paralysis is the core ADHD challenge - users need help STARTING tasks, not organizing them. AI-powered task breakdown (like Goblin Tools Magic ToDo) helps overcome executive dysfunction by making the first step immediately actionable.
[1] (personal) Task systems should HIDE overwhelming backlogs, not display them. Visual clutter physically degrades neural efficiency in ADHD brains (Yale 2024). Focus interfaces on 'What's next?' not 'What's pending?'
[2] (personal) ACT's cognitive defusion ('I'm having the thought that...') is more effective for ADHD emotional dysregulation than CBT-style cognitive challenging. Create distance from thoughts rather than arguing with them.
[3] (personal) Body doubling is among the most effective ADHD productivity strategies - Focusmate reports 90%+ reduction in procrastination. External social presence activates accountability circuits more effectively than any notification. Design for social presence features.
[4] (personal) ADHD users value simplicity over feature richness - successful apps have single-purpose interfaces rather than kitchen-sink approaches. This contradicts typical productivity app wisdom.
[5] (personal) 'Eat the Frog' (tackle hardest task first) often backfires for ADHD brains - creates paralysis if task feels impossible. Alternative: start with quick wins to build momentum.
[6] (personal) Email avoidance is specifically exacerbated by RSD (Rejection Sensitive Dysphoria) and social evaluation fears, not just task aversion. System design should reduce perceived social stakes through templates and AI assistance.
[7] (personal) Shame vs guilt distinction is critical for ADHD accountability systems - shame triggers avoidance/defensiveness while guilt motivates repair. ADHD users are particularly vulnerable to shame spirals due to RSD.
[8] (personal) Time blindness is a fundamental ADHD deficit - task systems must explicitly externalize time perception through visual cues, timers, and deadline urgencies.
[9] (personal) Gamification requires immediate feedback for ADHD brains - instant dopamine hits (growing trees, happy animations) work while delayed rewards (long streaks, leaderboards) fail. Design for immediate gratification.
[10] (personal) Context engineering is replacing prompt engineering as the key AI skill. Treat the AI's 'attention budget' as scarce and design systems that provide right information at right time. Real power comes from architecting the information ecosystem, not crafting individual prompts.
[11] (system) Airtable checkbox fields have parenthetical suffixes like 'Salary Range (Checkbox)' - use exact field names from schema documentation when building integrations.
[12] (system) When integrating Zo app tools (Airtable, Google Drive, Gmail), scripts should output instructions/logs for the tools rather than directly calling them. The orchestrator executes tool calls. This decouples skills from app tool implementation details.
[13] (system) Pulse deposit learnings can have multiple formats - list of objects with 'text' field, list of strings, or single strings. Extraction code must handle all three formats and skip malformed JSON gracefully.
[14] (system) Code output format and schema must be designed together - mismatches (e.g., flat array vs wrapped object) cause integration failures. Validate schema compatibility before implementation.
[15] (system) Making schema fields nullable allows graceful migration of legacy data without breaking existing records.
[16] (personal) Gamification follows predictable 2-4 week failure pattern due to novelty wear-off. Requires refresh mechanisms and variable rewards to maintain engagement beyond initial dopamine spike.
[17] (personal) Email filing paradox: folders needed for retrieval but filing requires high executive function at moment of receipt, creating the Piler vs Filer trap for ADHD users.
[18] (personal) ADHD market fragmentation: users juggle 3-5 apps (task breakdown, focus, gamification, tasks) because no single tool combines mechanisms effectively. Design for integration of core mechanisms rather than single-purpose tools.
[19] (system) Verify existing implementations match architecture requirements before starting work - existing code may use different patterns (e.g., SQLite vs JSON state file) requiring overwrite rather than extension.
[20] (system) Field naming inconsistencies (name vs skill_name, level_required vs required_level) should be caught by validation tests early in the pipeline, not at integration time.
[21] (system) task_registry.py is the canonical interface for N5/task-system - all code should import from it rather than direct SQL access. Domain and project auto-creation happens automatically when creating tasks.
[22] (personal) ADHD brains respond to personal interest-based theming (e.g., Arsenal FC) - leverages existing passion rather than adding cognitive load. Personalization beats sophistication for ADHD users.
[23] (system) LLM responses via /zo/ask often include markdown code fences around JSON or truncate mid-object. Use robust regex extraction that handles triple-backtick wrappers, extra content after valid JSON, and attempts to close incomplete objects/arrays.
[24] (system) Zo API calls can timeout at 120s during long extractions. Use 180s timeout with retry mechanism for content-heavy requests.
[25] (system) Cannot JOIN across SQLite databases. When working with multiple .db files, query each separately and merge results in Python.
[26] (system) Scheduled agents needing reply handling require dual trigger design: (1) scheduled run for initial action, (2) SMS/email reply trigger for follow-up processing. Same prompt handles both based on context detection (has reply content or not).
[27] (system) SMS interface cannot handle complex multi-option selection (domain/project pickers) due to character limits. Solution: mark items as 'pending_review' status and require browser-based completion for complex workflows.
[28] (system) Fillout API stores emoji-labeled responses as full text strings (e.g., '5 = 🚀 Excited / All-in'). Parse with pattern 'N = emoji Text' to extract numeric scores. Also: screening questions may not filter API responses - filter ineligible responses manually.
[29] (system) Contradiction detector: single-sided negation detection (one text has 'never/not/no' while semantically similar text doesn't) catches contradictions that paired patterns miss. Use similarity threshold of 0.7+ to avoid false positives.
[30] (system) Pulse safety checks (impossibility audit, prompt lint, contradiction detection, plateau detection) must use try/except for graceful fallback - detectors may not be available in all environments.
[31] (system) Pattern matching for work completion detection works better with standalone word patterns ('drafted', 'saved', 'created', 'built') than complex noun-dependent patterns. Use flexible word boundary detection.
[32] (personal) 100% uniform sentiment responses in surveys indicate selection bias, tribal signaling, or flawed question design - sentiment data is effectively broken and cannot support correlation analysis.
[33] (personal) 'Scratching the surface' is not a beginner state but a permanent condition for anyone staying current with AI - curriculum and self-assessment should reflect this reality.
[34] (system) SQLite column types matter for cross-table operations. INTEGER IDs must be converted to TEXT strings when storing in tables with TEXT ID columns, and vice versa when querying.
[35] (system) /zo/ask API ignores output_format parameter and returns natural language regardless of JSON schema requests. Parse semantic response instead of expecting structured JSON.
[36] (system) Batch API operations (migrations, bulk processing) run 3-4x slower than estimated due to API latency. Budget 3.5 items/sec for OpenAI calls, not theoretical throughput.
[37] (system) Temp directories get cleaned up before async operations complete. Use persistent storage paths (e.g., N5/data/) instead of temp directories for any data that must survive async processing.
[38] (system) SQLite database initialization: Use Python's sqlite3 module directly (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) rather than shell commands like 'sqlite3 < schema.sql'. Shell approach can leave empty DB files causing 'no such table' errors.
[39] (system) Pipeline pre-flight validation: Validate data alignment BEFORE execution. Check that input data (e.g., decomposer output) matches target context (e.g., correct employer). Misalignment causes silent failures.
[40] (system) gather_thread_context() is the single source of truth for context data. Wrapper scripts should only add presentation-layer flags for LLM consumption. Cleanly separates mechanics (data gathering) from semantics (interpretation).
[41] (personal) Evidence sufficiency framework: Strong (production work), Moderate (pre-production/indirect), Weak (educational/tangential), Not Found (no evidence). Tool mentions without project evidence = Weak. Stack mismatch = Not Found even for strong generalists.
[42] (personal) Differentiators vs table stakes: Generic soft skills ('team player', 'communicator') are table stakes, NOT differentiators. Only include: technical rarity, unusual skill combinations, domain expertise, achievement signals, or culture resonance.
[43] (personal) GitHub profile activity cadence (recent engagement) matters more than total historical numbers. Consistent recent commits signal active developer; 100 commits/year with no activity in 6 months signals lapsed engagement.
[44] (personal) Data quality warnings: When analysis detects anomalies (100% uniform sentiment, small sample sizes), add explicit warnings section to dashboards. Uniform max values often indicate tribal signaling, not actual sentiment.
[45] (system) Image generation alignment: Inspect tailwind.config/CSS variables for specific color names (e.g., 'zinc-900', 'sky-400') to align generated image aesthetics with UI theme.
[46] (system) ZO_CLIENT_IDENTITY_TOKEN may not be available in all execution contexts (e.g., scheduled agents, certain API calls). Add fallback error handling for integrations requiring this token.
[47] (system) SQLite embeddings stored as BLOB in vectors table (not separate embeddings table). Migration scripts must decode/encode float32 arrays, not just copy rows. Schema: block_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, embedding BLOB NOT NULL.
[48] (system) Path sanitization patterns must account for: end of string ($), various separators (/\s), and quoted contexts ("'). Use character class like ([/\s"'\)]|$) to capture and preserve context.
[49] (system) When executing a Drop, extract build_slug and drop_id from drop brief frontmatter and pass to session_state_manager.py via --build and --worker-num flags. Without these, router misroutes to thread-close instead of drop-close.
[50] (system) Meeting folder state: [R] marker is NOT in folder names. State tracked in manifest.json (status field). Scripts should check manifest.json['status'] instead of folder name patterns.
[51] (system) Build deposits must verify ALL artifacts listed in drop spec exist before marking complete. Missing artifacts discovered later require rework. Quality gate should check content existence, not just brief presence.
[52] (personal) Analysis synthesis should use explicit Level Upper insight markers (🔮) to distinguish novel AI perspectives from standard findings. Helps readers identify where AI added unique value.
[53] (system) Pulse learnings CLI: use 'list <build_slug>' to read build learnings. There is no 'read' subcommand despite documentation references.
[54] (system) Word overlap matching for imprecision detection: 40% threshold on expanded word sets works better than direct substring matching which is too rigid.
[55] (system) GitHub force-push preserves content but destroys history. Always verify existing directory structure before copying. Consider workflow warnings on low commit count detection.
[56] (system) Git-based change detection works well for incremental exports but requires tracking state (last_export.json) to identify what changed since last run.

TASK: Identify which indices are duplicates and should be merged.
When merging, preserve the category of the first (representative) index.

OUTPUT FORMAT (strict JSON, no markdown):
{
  "merge_groups": [
    [0, 5, 12],
    [3, 8]
  ],
  "keep_as_is": [1, 2, 4, 6, 7, 9, 10, 11]
}

merge_groups: Arrays of indices to merge (first index is the representative)
keep_as_is: Indices that are unique

Return valid JSON only.","timestamp":"2026-02-06T20:19:45.211837Z","part_kind":"user-prompt"}],"timestamp":null,"instructions":"","kind":"request","run_id":null,"metadata":null}
